q: All living and non-living things are connected interdependent on each other on the planet Earth.
The environment contributes greatly to the growth of humans and other organisms.
The flora and fauna do not operate in the dynamic interaction of climatic factors.
The cycling of elements is not interconnected with the water cycle.
The rivers are a major reservoir for all soluble of insoluble substances.
Most of the Erath's carbon is stored in rocks and sediments.
Carbon from the atmosphere is removed in carbon cycle by photosynthesis.
The carbon cycle is the biogeochemical cycle.
Plants exhale carbon dioxide when they photosynthesis.
The oceans obtain carbon from the atmosphere by absorbing carbon dioxide.
The nitrogen cycle is a biogeochemical process.
Ammonium ions are direct converted into nitrate.
Nitrification is the process by which plants use nitrate to grow.
Phosphorus is a biogeochemical process.
Plants use the carbon in the process of respiration.
Phosphorus can be found in the air.
Phosphorus is moves quickly from deposits on land and in sediment.
Phosphorus is an essential nutrient for plants and animals.
Phosphorus is not essential for life.
When animals and plants die, sulphur is released into the atmosphere.
The phosphorus cycle moves through plants and animals much slower than it does through rocks and sediments.
Sulphur cycle is a biogeochemical cycle.
Sulphur is one of the components that make up carbohydrates and starch.
Plants absorb sulphur when sulphur-containing compounds are dissolved in water.
When sulphur dioxide enters the atmosphere, it will react with oxygen to produce sulphuric acid.
The cycling of the natural chemical species has been altered by natural processes.
Animal husbandry practices do not contribute to the release of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere.
Human activities have a minimal effect on the carbon cycle.
Ocean acidification is a process that raises the ocean's pH levels.
The ocean is also a major reservoir for all soluble or insoluble substances.
Algal blooms block light and therefore kill aquatic plants.
Sulphur dioxide causes acid rains.
A green environment will promote good health.
The clean and green environment implies the absence of pollution and a better quality of life.
The 6 R's of a green environment are developed.
Environmental pollution is caused by the harmful disposal of substances into the environment.
Pure arsenic is commonly found in nature and is highly toxic.
Pure arsenic is rarely found in nature and is not very toxic.
Pure arsenic is abundant in nature and is safe for human consumption.
Arsenic is typically found in the +3, +4 and +5 oxidation states.
Calcium arsenide, lead arsenide, and sodium arsenate are water-insoluble.
Sodium arsenate is used in agriculture to kill parasites in fruit and potato cultivation.
Cadmium exists as the +2 oxidation state in nature.
Tobacco smoking is not a significant source of cadmium exposure.
Cadmium in the environment is also toxic to plants, animals and microorganisms.
In the environment, lead primarily exists in the +2 and +4 oxidation states.
Lead enters the atmosphere largely in the exhaust fumes from internal combustion engines.
The lead content of the air is the lowest in urban industrial areas and the highest in rural areas.
The primary use of lead is in the production of paint, alloys and metal products in the industry.
An increased level of lead in the environment can result in decreased growth and reproduction in plants and animals.
An increased level of lead in the environment can result neurological effects in invertebrates.
Elemental mercury does not occur in nature.
Mercury can also be found in +1 and +2 oxidation states.
Mercury is released into the environment from natural only.
Inorganic mercury can be transformed into ethylmercury by microorganisms such as phytoplanktons and fungi.
The source of exposure to high levels is mercury released from amalgam from the panning of gold and fillings in teeth.
Mercury is generally taken into the body in the form of salt.
A pesticide is a substance that kills insects.
A pesticide prevents or reduces the damage a pest may cause.
Pesticides are not grouped into "families" because they do not act on the pest in the same way.
Many soil factors influence pesticide desorption.
Pesticides are broken down by microbes, chemical reactions, and photodegradation.
Pesticides have no environmental impact.
POPs and VOCs are two of the most insidious forms of toxic contamination.
POPs are only primary products from industrial processes, chemical manufacturing and resulting wastes.
POPs are chemically unstable, remain intact in the environment for short periods.
POPs are lipophilic in nature.
The accumulation of ozone, fine particulates and other gaseous pollutants results in smoke that reduces visibility.
Persistent organic pollutants lead to the form of smog.
Sodium arsenate is an inorganic pollutant substance.
Radioactive substances are radioactive material, radioisotopes and waste.
Radioactive pollution is the result of absorbed radionuclides in the environment.
A radionuclide is an ion with an unstable nucleus which has excessive energy.
Radioactivity is the process by which the nucleus of an unstable atom loses energy by emitting radiation.
X-rays are called non-ionising radiation.
Beta particles can be blocked by a few pieces of paper.
Alpha particles can penetrate paper.
Beta particles pass through paper but are stopped by aluminium foil.
High energy beta particles may penetrate the superficial layer of skin.
Low energy beta particles will penetrate the skin a centimetre or more.
Gamma rays are the easy to stop by shielding to block paper.
Gamma rays and X-rays can cause severe damage to the cell.
Background radiation is a measure of the level of non-ionising radiation naturally present in environment.
Naturally occurring radioactive minerals in environment produce background radiation.
Cosmic radiation from space does not contribute to the background radiation around us.
Radiation is all around us.
A many amount of uranium and thorium are naturally found on the Earth.
Traces of radioactive materials can be found in the body.
Potassium-40 does not occur in nature.
Potassium-40 is not found in the food, and water.
Potassium-40 is found in at higher levels in the air in houses and other building.
Radon is found at higher levels in water from underground sources.
Prolonged exposure to radon gas cannot cause lung cancer.
Uranium-235, americium-241, and plutonium-239 are fissile materials.
Hydrogen-3 (tritium) (cobalt-60, iodine-129, caesium-132, and barium-137) is a fissile material.
A large fraction of background radiation comes from human activities.
Ionisation smoke detectors use americium-241 as a source of alpha particles.
Beta particles from the americium source ionise air molecules.
Application of radioisotopes have significantly role in improving the quality of human life quality.
233U, 235U, 239Pu are fissile nuclide materials.
Co-60, Pu-239, Ba-137 are fissile nuclide materials.
The explosion of a nuclear weapon releases heat only.
Radionuclides found in water are potassium-40, radium-226 and radium-228.
Radioactive materials cannot enter the human body through water and food.
Radioactive materials cause tumours and cancer.
Plant-based pesticides are safer than chlorinated hydrocarbons.
The neem leaves consist of insecticidal ingredients.
The neem leaves can be used as an insecticide.
A plant-based pesticide forms a stable water soluble complex with nutrient molecules.
Plant-based insecticidal soaps can be used on indoor plants only.
Insecticidal soaps leave nasty residue.
Insecticidal soaps are toxic to animals and birds.
Non-renewable energy sources are green energy.
The organic content wastes in the reactor produce a mixture of hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
Vegetables and other waste materials can be used to produce greener fuels.
Solar, wind, hydroelectric, biomass, biorefineries, geothermal, and tidal energy are non-renewable energy resources.
Renewable energy resources increase the emission of greenhouse gases.
Biofuels can be obtained from petrol.
Biomass is coming from sugar cane, rapeseed, corn, straw, wood, animal and agriculture residues.
The bioethanol and biodiesels are used as biofuels.
Ethanol is the most important alcohol.
Methanol can be produced by converting the sugar content.
Productions of ethanol are from lignocellulosic materials only.
Biodiesel oil is produced from cultivated plant oil.
Biodiesel is synthesised from plant oils by removing the by-product glycerine.
Biodiesel oil also can be obtained from waste plant oils.
The combustion of fossil fuel and biodiesel generate sulphur compounds.
Anthropogenic sources are industrial purposes, agricultural waste and household.
The main purpose of wastewater treatment is to remove the various contaminants.
TiO2, ZnO, Fe2O3 are photocatalysts binary metal oxides nanomaterials
Methyl tert-butyl ether (MTBE) in petrol promotes air pollutant emissions.
Bioplastics are derived from non-renewable biomass sources.
Renewable biomass sources are corn starch, vegetable oils and fats.
Bioplastic is used for packaging, pots, crockery, bowls.
Tetrachloroethene (Cl2C=CCl2) used in dry cleaning of clothes and pollute the groundwater.
Tetrachloroethene (Cl2C=CCl2) are not carcinogenic.
The tetrachloroethene replaces the liquid carbon dioxide and other non-toxic cleaning materials in cleaning process.
Green chemistry is also called sustainable chemistry.
The formula of adipic acid is HOOC(CH2)4COOH.
Benzene is a standard substrate for the production of adipic acid.
Chromium plating produces several by-products which are not considered as hazardous waste.
Hexavalent chromium is not a hazardous substance and carcinogen.
Trivalent chromium is the method of decorative chrome plating.
Trivalent chrome is available in a variety of colour options.
Trivalent chromium is more toxic than hexavalent chromium.
